title: Magnificently creamy Middle Eastern lamb Tagine
description: A rich and creamy one-pot dish featuring tender lamb, madras paste, and a shio-koji cure, perfect for a special occasion.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it starts to soften, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the madras paste and shio-koji and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the dried apricots, chicken broth, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2.5 hours.
Stir in the heavy cream and continue to simmer, uncovered, for 30 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
